name: GasLimitManipulationAttack
description: This attack exploits the possibility of manipulating the gas limit to cause a denial of service or execute unintended logic within the EtherStore contract. By crafting transactions that consume maximum gas limits, an attacker can either prevent other critical operations or trigger unintended fallback logic within the contract. This manipulation can lead to scenarios where legitimate withdrawals or deposits are blocked, or where the contract falls back to less secure states, allowing the attacker to exploit these weakened conditions to siphon funds.
steps: Analyze the EtherStore contract to identify functions sensitive to gas limits and potential fallback logic.
Craft transactions with maximum gas usage to force the contract into fallback states or prevent other users from executing necessary transactions.
Monitor the contract's behavior under high gas consumption conditions to identify any fallback logic that can be exploited.
Execute transactions to exploit the identified fallback logic or take advantage of blocked operations to withdraw excess funds.
expected_impact: The attacker can cause disruption in the contract operations, leading to scenarios where they can withdraw more funds than legitimate or prevent other users from accessing the contract's functionalities.
